Chrysanthemum pulchellum is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the yellow chrysanthemum. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum pulchellum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum pulchellum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive discomfort.
